metadata.title: Azents | Self-host managed agents
metadata.description: The open-source control plane for managed agents in your cloud.

hero.headline: Managed agents, inside your cloud.
hero.subheadline: Azents is an open-source control plane for remote agent runtimes.
hero.supporting: Keep sessions, tools, permissions, and execution state next to your repos and private network.

architecture.title: One control plane. Your runtime boundary.
architecture.body: Azents keeps sessions in the control plane and runs workspace operations through runtimes in your infrastructure.

diagram.label: User and team connect to Azents Engine, which coordinates Runtime inside your cloud or on-prem environment.

team.title: User / Team
team.description: Starts durable sessions against real workspaces.

engine.title: Azents Engine
engine.description: Owns sessions, state, orchestration, and handover.

runtime.title: Runtime
runtime.description: Runs tools and workspace operations in your infrastructure.

runtime.title: Runtime
runtime.body: Execution environment for code, files, and tools.

session.title: Session
session.body: Durable work context for long-running agent tasks.

architectureDeepDive.title: The parts that make remote agents durable.
architectureDeepDive.body: Azents separates the session loop, runtime boundary, and provider-specific execution so agent work can survive real infrastructure.

controls.keyboardLabel: Architecture carousel. Use left and right arrow keys, Home, and End to move slides.

architectureDeepDive.gestureHint: Scroll, swipe, or use arrow keys. {current} / {total}

decoupledLoop.imageAlt: Abstract architecture diagram showing a durable session loop separated from runtime execution.
decoupledLoop.title: An independent session loop, decoupled from runtime execution.
decoupledLoop.body: Engine owns the agent loop and durable events. Runtime is called only when work needs files, tools, shell, or compute.

diagram.sessionBody: The run can be replayed, inspected, and resumed outside any one runtime.
diagram.loopBody: Reads events, asks the model what to do next, emits the next event.
diagram.runtimeBody: Runs workspace operations and can be replaced without losing the session.

providers.imageAlt: Abstract architecture diagram showing a runtime adapter branching into multiple provider backends.
providers.title: Providers sit behind one runtime adapter.
providers.body: Kubernetes is first. The same adapter shape can map to local machines, E2B, or custom execution backends as the project grows.

diagram.adapterBody: The Engine asks for workspace-scoped execution. Providers decide how that execution is provisioned.

longRunning.imageAlt: Abstract architecture diagram showing a long-running agent timeline with durable state and handoff.
longRunning.title: Long-running agents need durable state.
longRunning.body: Runs should keep their history, progress, and handoff point even when a tab closes, a worker moves, or execution is reprovisioned.

start.title: Start
start.body: A session begins against a real workspace.

disconnect.title: Disconnect
disconnect.body: The client can leave without ending the run.

handover.title: Handoff
handover.body: Another Engine worker can continue from the event log.

resume.title: Resume
resume.body: Progress is visible and the run continues from durable state.

problem.title: Close the laptop. The agent keeps working.
problem.body: Durable runtimes keep sessions alive near the workspace.
problem.panelBody: A run should survive disconnects, show progress, and resume without rebuilding local state.

sessions.title: Runs keep moving
sessions.body: Work continues after the tab, SSH session, or laptop is gone.

scaling.title: Workspace-native
scaling.body: Execute near the repo, files, shell, and task state.

failover.title: Resume later
failover.body: Pick up the same session without rehydrating your machine.

observability.title: Visible progress
observability.body: See tool calls, command output, diffs, and blockers.

permissions.title: Team-visible
permissions.body: A run belongs to the workspace, not one developer's laptop.

state.title: Right-sized runtime
state.body: Give heavier jobs the CPU, tools, and time they need.

problem.footnote: Remote execution answers how agents keep working. Self-hosting answers where they run.

selfHosted.title: Run managed agents as part of your cloud.
selfHosted.body: Keep runtimes close to repos, credentials, internal APIs, and audit trails.
selfHosted.thesis: Azents gives teams a self-hosted control plane for operating remote agent runs inside their own infrastructure.

bootstrapping.title: Azents is already using its own harness.
bootstrapping.body: Azents is now used to develop Azents. The coding harness handles repositories, edits, shell, PRs, CI, review feedback, and verification.

capabilities.repo: read the repository before changing it
capabilities.editing: edit files while preserving a working diff
capabilities.github: open PRs and address review feedback

features.title: Managed-agent operations, self-hosted.
features.body: Not another chat UI. The operating layer for agents that work near real systems.

sessions.title: Managed sessions
sessions.body: Long-running work contexts with durable history.

history.title: Event history
history.body: Execution events for replay, audit, and inspection.

separation.title: Engine / Runtime split
separation.body: Scale orchestration separately from workspace execution.

permissions.title: Tool permissions
permissions.body: Control what an agent can read, run, call, and change.

providers.title: Runtime providers
providers.body: Run execution environments with Docker and Kubernetes-oriented providers.

harness.title: Coding harness
harness.body: Development workflows for repositories, PRs, and CI loops.

tools.title: Self-hosted model
tools.body: Designed for private cloud, on-prem, and regulated environments.

roadmap.title: Built for teams operating agents close to real systems.
roadmap.body: For platform engineers, infra teams, AI product builders, and organizations that need self-hosted control.

finalCta.title: Self-host managed agents.
finalCta.body: Run agent sessions, runtimes, and tools where your work already lives.

footer.tagline: Azents is open-source infrastructure for self-hosted managed agents.

fontPreview.headline: Run managed agents in your cloud.
fontPreview.subheadline: Azents is the open-source control plane for managed-agent sessions, runtimes, tools, and execution state.
fontPreview.supporting: Keep the runtime near your code, files, permissions, and private systems.
fontPreview.pageBody: These use local/system font stacks, so the rendered line at the bottom shows what the browser actually picked.
